title: feat: Switch to enum queue mode, add 'replace' mode.
body:
Follow up to https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/pull/1879

Kind of janky because of the fact that job queue tasks are split up before the client- I make clear-queue a property of the first job.

Not sure if the enum is in the right place.

Tentatively planning to implement ctrl-click as "Alternate queue mode".

step_plan.summary: Replace the boolean queue insertion flag with an enum-based queue mode and add a new 'replace' mode that clears queued work once per generation action. Update the model enqueue pipeline and queue UI to support the new modes while preserving correct behavior when a single action is split into multiple jobs.

steps.operation: Introduce enum-based queue mode property and migrate from boolean queue_front; implement replace-mode prelude handling for a generation action
steps.reason: generate() is the top-level user entry point; switching to an enum here centralizes interpretation of queue policy (back/front/replace) and allows replace semantics (clear queue once per user action) to run before batch splitting/enqueueing begins.
steps.side_effects: Requires updating any callers/tests that set/read Model.queue_front; persisted settings may need migration/compat (old boolean -> new enum). Replace mode may clear queued work more aggressively than before; ensure UI and status indicators remain consistent.

steps.operation: Apply replace semantics exactly once across batch splitting (clear only once, not per split job) and propagate chosen QueueMode through the enqueue pipeline
steps.reason: enqueue_jobs() performs batch splitting into multiple Job submissions; this is where you must ensure replace clears the backend/local queued jobs only once for the overall action (e.g., before the first split job) rather than repeating per job, which would be incorrect/janky.
steps.side_effects: Ordering changes may affect timing (clear_queue vs first enqueue). If the backend clear is async, you may need to wait/sequence to prevent races where old jobs still run. Local job list manipulation must remain consistent with Queue UI.

steps.operation: Translate QueueMode into client enqueue parameters (front/back) and ensure replace-specific behavior does not leak into per-job enqueue calls
steps.reason: _enqueue_job() is the per-job bridge to client.enqueue(..., front=...). After replacing queue_front with QueueMode, this method must map enum -> boolean front/back and avoid triggering replace actions per job (replace should be handled once upstream).
steps.side_effects: Any code paths that assumed a bool property may break; enqueue ordering will change when QueueMode=FRONT. If QueueMode is extended later, this mapping becomes a central compatibility point.

steps.operation: Refactor/extend cancel(queued=True) logic for reuse by replace mode to clear remote queue and reconcile local queued jobs consistently
steps.reason: cancel(queued=True) already contains the canonical logic to clear the backend queue and remove locally queued jobs. Replace mode should reuse the same behavior to avoid duplicated logic and to ensure local/remote queue state stays in sync.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral changes to cancel could impact the existing Cancel UI actions. Must ensure cancel(active vs queued) semantics remain intact; tests around job list updates may need adjustment.

steps.operation: Update QueuePopup binding to use the new enum property (queue_mode) instead of the old boolean (queue_front)
steps.reason: QueuePopup.model currently binds a combo to Model.queue_front. With enum-based queue modes, binding must point to Model.queue_mode and correctly round-trip selection changes into the persisted Property.
steps.side_effects: UI state persistence changes (stored value becomes enum/string/int depending on serialization). Any other widgets referencing queue_front will need updating; ensure combo selection updates do not break when loading older configs.

steps.operation: Populate QueuePopup combo entries for enum modes and add the new 'replace' mode option with appropriate labels and ordering
steps.reason: The UI must expose all queue modes (e.g., Back, Front, Replace). __init__ constructs the combo items; it must be updated to reflect the enum values and include the new replace mode so users can select it.
steps.side_effects: Label changes can affect translations/strings if i18n exists. Users may see a new default or reordered options; ensure default selection matches previous behavior (likely 'back').

steps.operation: Ensure backend queue clearing is correctly sequenced/awaited so replace mode can safely clear before first enqueue without race conditions
steps.reason: Replace mode relies on clear_queue being reliable and ordered relative to subsequent enqueues. If clear_queue is asynchronous or buffered, adjust implementation/contract so a clear request completes (or is guaranteed ordered) before the next enqueue proceeds.
steps.side_effects: May change timing and concurrency characteristics of queue clearing for existing Cancel/Clear Queue actions. Could require updating any callers that assumed fire-and-forget semantics; might necessitate additional tests around ordering and queue state.
